questions.question: In the physical verification phase of Integrated Circuit (IC) design, what is the primary purpose of Design Rule Checking (DRC)?
options: To ensure that the physical layout adheres to manufacturing constraints and geometric rules.
To verify that the circuit's timing requirements are met under various operating conditions.
To confirm the functional equivalence between the Register-Transfer Level (RTL) and gate-level netlists.
To extract parasitic capacitances and resistances from the layout for post-layout simulation.
To optimize the power consumption of the design by identifying and removing redundant gates.

questions.question: Which computer architecture concept describes a system where instructions and data are stored in separate memory spaces, allowing simultaneous access to both?
options: Von Neumann Architecture
Harvard Architecture
MIMD (Multiple Instruction, Multiple Data) Architecture
CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) Architecture
Superscalar Architecture

questions.question: When transferring data between two asynchronous clock domains in a digital IC, which circuit type is primarily used to mitigate metastability issues and ensure reliable data transfer?
options: Transparent Latches
A First-In, First-Out (FIFO) Buffer
A Synchronizer (e.g., a two-flip-flop synchronizer)
A Parallel-In, Serial-Out (PISO) Shift Register
A Combinational Logic Decoder

questions.question: In Design-for-Testability (DFT) methodologies for digital ASICs, what is the primary purpose of converting sequential elements (flip-flops) into 'scan flip-flops' and connecting them to form scan chains?
options: To reduce the dynamic power consumption of the chip during normal operation.
To enable the chip to operate at significantly higher clock frequencies.
To improve the test coverage by allowing internal states to be easily observed and controlled during manufacturing testing.
To implement a Built-In Self-Test (BIST) mechanism directly into the design.
To facilitate faster logic synthesis and place-and-route by simplifying sequential logic.

questions.question: In a multi-core processor system, what does a 'Release Consistency' memory model primarily allow compared to a 'Sequential Consistency' model?
options: It strictly enforces that all memory operations appear to execute in program order across all cores, simplifying programmer reasoning.
It allows a limited reordering of memory operations (specifically writes related to 'release' fences) to improve performance, while still providing synchronization guarantees.
It ensures that only read operations are globally ordered, allowing write operations to be reordered freely without any synchronization primitives.
It uses a centralized arbiter to ensure that only one core can access a specific memory location at any given time, regardless of the operation type.
It enables a core to unilaterally update its private cache without notifying other cores until an explicit flush operation is performed.
